revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)]][[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)]][[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)]][[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677855]] Wikidata name: Koganeyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11677855]] Wikidata name: Koganeyama Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:15, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Koganeyama Jinja | native_name = 黄金山神社 | native_name_lang = Japanese | image = File:Koganeyama-jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden of Koganeyama Jinja | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.55982778|N|141.13914722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Koganeyama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Kanayamahiko|WD=Q4387856}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = c. 767 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 23 Koganegu-mae, Wakuya-machi, Tōda-gun, Miyagi-ken | website = {{URL|http://wakuya-koganeyama.info/index2.html}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} | festival = September 15 | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} | location = {{ill|Wakuya|WD=Q1155902}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Koganeyama Jinja'''|黄金山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Wakuya, Miyagi|Wakuya]] {{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|WD=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It claims to have been built on the site of the first [[:en:Gold mine|Gold mine]] in Japan, and is protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162061|title=黄金山産金遺跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
The main festival of the shrine is held annually on September 15.
==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Koganeyama Jinja is {{nihongo|''Kanayama-hiko-no-kami''|金山彦神}}.
==History== The foundation of the Koganeyama Jinja and the date of discovery of gold in the region are unknown. In 740 AD, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} was constructing the [[Tōdai-ji|Nara Daibutsu]], a call was sent out to all provinces requesting the donation of gold for the statue. The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|WD=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|WD=Q3057942|lt=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)}} *[https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki07.html Miyagi Prefectural official site] *[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020472 Miyagi Shinto Shrine Association] {{in lang|ja}}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including--> {{Authority control}}

The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Koganeyama Jinja | native_name = 黄金山神社 | native_name_lang = Japanese | image = File:Koganeyama-jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden of Koganeyama Jinja | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.55982778|N|141.13914722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Koganeyama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Kanayamahiko|WD=Q4387856}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = c. 767 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 23 Koganegu-mae, Wakuya-machi, Tōda-gun, Miyagi-ken | website = {{URL|http://wakuya-koganeyama.info/index2.html}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} | festival = September 15 | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} | location = {{ill|Wakuya|WD=Q1155902}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Koganeyama Jinja'''|黄金山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Wakuya, Miyagi|Wakuya]] {{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|WD=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It claims to have been built on the site of the first [[:en:Gold mine|Gold mine]] in Japan, and is protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162061|title=黄金山産金遺跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
The main festival of the shrine is held annually on September 15.
==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Koganeyama Jinja is {{nihongo|''Kanayama-hiko-no-kami''|金山彦神}}.
==History== The foundation of the Koganeyama Jinja and the date of discovery of gold in the region are unknown. In 740 AD, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} was constructing the [[Tōdai-ji|Nara Daibutsu]], a call was sent out to all provinces requesting the donation of gold for the statue. The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|WD=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|WD=Q3057942|lt=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}

The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|WD=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|WD=Q3057942|lt=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)}} *[https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki07.html Miyagi Prefectural official site] *[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020472 Miyagi Shinto Shrine Association] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox religious building | name = Koganeyama Jinja | native_name = 黄金山神社 | native_name_lang = Japanese | image = File:Koganeyama-jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden of Koganeyama Jinja | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.55982778|N|141.13914722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Koganeyama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Kanayamahiko|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | founded_by = | established = c. 767 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 23 Koganegu-mae, Wakuya-machi, Tōda-gun, Miyagi-ken | website = {{URL|http://wakuya-koganeyama.info/index2.html}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} | location = {{ill|Wakuya|WD=Q1155902|1=Wakuya}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Koganeyama Jinja'''|黄金山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Wakuya, Miyagi|Wakuya]] {{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|WD=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District|1=Tōda District, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It claims to have been built on the site of the first [[:en:Gold mine|Gold mine]] in Japan, and is protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162061|title=黄金山産金遺跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
The main festival of the shrine is held annually on September 15.
==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Koganeyama Jinja is {{nihongo|''Kanayama-hiko-no-kami''|金山彦神}}.
==History== The foundation of the Koganeyama Jinja and the date of discovery of gold in the region are unknown. In 740 AD, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} was constructing the [[Tōdai-ji|Nara Daibutsu]], a call was sent out to all provinces requesting the donation of gold for the statue. The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|WD=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje|1=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|WD=Q3057942|lt=Ryō|1=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University|1=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station|1=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line|1=Ishinomaki Line}}.

{{Infobox religious building | name = Koganeyama Jinja | native_name = 黄金山神社 | native_name_lang = Japanese | image = File:Koganeyama-jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden of Koganeyama Jinja | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.55982778|N|141.13914722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Koganeyama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Kanayamahiko|WD=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | founded_by = | established = c. 767 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 23 Koganegu-mae, Wakuya-machi, Tōda-gun, Miyagi-ken | website = {{URL|http://wakuya-koganeyama.info/index2.html}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} | location = {{ill|Wakuya|WD=Q1155902|1=Wakuya}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Koganeyama Jinja'''|黄金山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Wakuya, Miyagi|Wakuya]] {{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|WD=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District|1=Tōda District, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|WD=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|WD=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It claims to have been built on the site of the first [[:en:Gold mine|Gold mine]] in Japan, and is protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162061|title=黄金山産金遺跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
The main festival of the shrine is held annually on September 15.
==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Koganeyama Jinja is {{nihongo|''Kanayama-hiko-no-kami''|金山彦神}}.
==History== The foundation of the Koganeyama Jinja and the date of discovery of gold in the region are unknown. In 740 AD, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} was constructing the [[Tōdai-ji|Nara Daibutsu]], a call was sent out to all provinces requesting the donation of gold for the statue. The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|WD=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|WD=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje|1=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|WD=Q3057942|lt=Ryō|1=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|WD=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University|1=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|WD=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|WD=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station|1=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|WD=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|WD=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line|1=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|WD=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|WD=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)}} *[https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki07.html Miyagi Prefectural official site] *[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020472 Miyagi Shinto Shrine Association] {{in lang|ja}}

Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|qid=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University|1=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|qid=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station|1=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|qid=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line|1=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|qid=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}
==References== {{Reflist}}
==External links== {{Commons category-inline|Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)}} *[https://www.pref.miyagi.jp/site/sitei/kuni-siseki07.html Miyagi Prefectural official site] *[http://www.miyagi-jinjacho.or.jp/jinja-search/detail.php?code=310020472 Miyagi Shinto Shrine Association] {{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)]][[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Koganeyama-jinja (Wakuya)]][[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Koganeyama Shrine]][[ja:黄金山神社 (涌谷町)]]
{{Infobox religious building | name = Koganeyama Jinja | native_name = 黄金山神社 | native_name_lang = Japanese | image = File:Koganeyama-jinja Haiden.JPG | caption = Haiden of Koganeyama Jinja | map_type = Japan Miyagi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|38.55982778|N|141.13914722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = Koganeyama Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}; {{ill|Kanayamahiko|qid=Q4387856|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}}; {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami}} | founded_by = | established = c. 767 AD | date_destroyed = | location = 23 Koganegu-mae, Wakuya-machi, Tōda-gun, Miyagi-ken | website = {{URL|http://wakuya-koganeyama.info/index2.html}} | architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = September 15 | footnotes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} | location = {{ill|Wakuya|qid=Q1155902|1=Wakuya}} }}
The {{nihongo|'''Koganeyama Jinja'''|黄金山神社}} is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} in the town of [[:en:Wakuya, Miyagi|Wakuya]] {{ill|Tōda District, Miyagi|en|Tōda District, Miyagi|qid=Q1131910|lt=Tōda District|1=Tōda District, Miyagi}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|en|Miyagi Prefecture|qid=Q47896|lt=Miyagi Prefecture|1=Miyagi Prefecture}} in the {{ill|Tōhoku region|en|Tōhoku region|qid=Q129465|lt=Tōhoku region|1=Tōhoku region}} of northern {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It claims to have been built on the site of the first [[:en:Gold mine|Gold mine]] in Japan, and is protected by the central government as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|National Historic Site]].<ref name= "Bunka">{{cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/162061|title=黄金山産金遺跡|work=Cultural Heritage Online|publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs|language=Japanese|accessdate=25 December 2016}}</ref>
The main festival of the shrine is held annually on September 15.
==Enshrined ''kami''== The primary ''[[kami]]'' of Koganeyama Jinja is {{nihongo|''Kanayama-hiko-no-kami''|金山彦神}}.
==History== The foundation of the Koganeyama Jinja and the date of discovery of gold in the region are unknown. In 740 AD, when {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|qid=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} was constructing the [[Tōdai-ji|Nara Daibutsu]], a call was sent out to all provinces requesting the donation of gold for the statue. The ruler of {{ill|Mutsu Province|en|Mutsu Province|qid=Q907389|lt=Mutsu Province|1=Mutsu Province}}, Kudara-no-konikishi Kyofuku (a descendant of King {{ill|Uija of Baekje|en|Uija of Baekje|qid=Q498030|lt=Uija of Baekje|1=Uija of Baekje}}), responded with 900 ''{{ill|Ryō|en|Ryō|qid=Q3057942|lt=Ryō|1=Ryō}}'' of gold (approximately 13 kilograms) from Tōda District, followed by an additional 900 ''ryō'' of gold sand two years later. Mention of a shrine at this location is made in the ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from 767 AD.
The shrine has a ''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' but no ''[[honden]]'', as the mountain to the rear of the shrine was regarded as the seat of the ''kami''.
The current ''[[Shinmei-zukuri]]'' style ''Haiden'' dates from 1909. Under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]], it was Ranked as a Prefectural Shrine.
During archaeological investigations conducted in 1957 by {{ill|Tohoku University|en|Tohoku University|qid=Q1062129|lt=Tohoku University|1=Tohoku University}}, the foundations of {{ill|Nara period|en|Nara period|qid=Q189178|lt=Nara period|1=Nara period}} structures and [[:en:Roof tile|Roof tile]]s were unearthed. The shrine was designated a Miyagi Prefectural Historic Site in 1959 and a National Historic Site in 1967. The shrine is a forty five minute walk from {{ill|Wakuya Station|en|Wakuya Station|qid=Q7961231|lt=Wakuya Station|1=Wakuya Station}} on the {{ill|East Japan Railway Company|en|East Japan Railway Company|qid=Q499071|lt=East Japan Railway Company|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|Ishinomaki Line|en|Ishinomaki Line|qid=Q1058854|lt=Ishinomaki Line|1=Ishinomaki Line}}.
==See also== * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines|en|List of Shinto shrines|qid=Q120679573|lt=List of Shinto shrines|1=List of Shinto shrines}} *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|qid=Q6573276|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Miyagi)}}
